extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIC Primož took the opening ITT by 6 seconds over ARANBURU Alex, savaging the flat final kilometers while BERNAL disappointed on the climb.
tactical_patterns: ARANBURU Alex beat pure climbers on hilly ITT, suggesting sprinters with power-to-weight can exploit technical parcours with limited climbing.
ROGLIC Primož gained decisive time in final 300m at 35mph, revealing pacing strategy that saved watts for flat finish.
BERNAL Egan lost 17 seconds to ARANBURU on 2.5km climb despite expectations, signaling form concerns from protected GC leader.

rider_signals.ARANBURU Alex: positive: Fast sprinter took 2nd overall and beat YATES Adam on climb, showing unexpected climbing form threatens pure climbers
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: negative: Lost 21 seconds to ROGLIC and surprisingly slow on climb segment, raising questions about GC form and Ineos protection hierarchy
rider_signals.CRADDOCK Lawson: positive: Fourth place and half-second off climb KOM shows EF domestique has unexpected TT form for support role
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primož: positive: Six-second margin gained entirely in final 300m demonstrates superior pacing and flat power reserves after climbing
rider_signals.YATES Adam: negative: Beaten by teammate VAN BAARLE Dylan and multiple non-climbers on hilly course suggests TT weakness worse than expected
